description:
From /critical-review of specled_-csg, consensus finding F5 [Medium; idioms+simplicity]. TreeHash.current/1 only File.rm's the temp index on success; the error path tap's cleanup_temp_reason/1 which is a no-op for every clause — temp index files leak into .git/specled-tmp on every failed run, and the function's name claims cleanup that never happens. Either actually delete the temp file on error or drop the dead function.
Files: lib/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash.ex:12-24,63-64.
Full report: /Users/mz/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split/20260722-062318-consolidated.md

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, run-2 W2)
Note: specled_-737 has landed on the epic branch — tree_hash.ex is now rewired onto the shared SpecLedEx.Evidence.Git module; line numbers in the ticket body predate that refactor.
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash.ex
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/git.ex (only if the error-path temp cleanup rightly belongs in the shared helper)
- test/specled_ex/evidence/** (tree_hash tests)
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (verification stubs only, if needed)
Out of scope (intent): fix = temp index actually deleted on error paths (or the dead cleanup_temp_reason/1 dropped with real cleanup wired); no other behavior change. Do not touch sync.ex/entry.ex (owned by specled_-7xz this wave). Any file outside these globs must be disclosed in IMPLEMENTED.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split - implement (worktree specled_-43y/tree-hash-temp-cleanup)
Log: n/a (interactive delegated worker, no separate log file)

Commit: 5cc6456
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash.ex, test/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Evidence.TreeHashTest "current removes the temporary index when a later git step fails"
Verification stubs flipped: none (no stub existed for this fix; requirement specled.evidence_store.tree_hash_mirrors_add_all already active)
Verification tier: change-scoped — test/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash_test.exs (3 tests, 0 failures), full mix test --stale (995 tests + 3 properties, 0 failures), mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean, mix format --check-formatted clean on touched files, mix spec.check --no-run-commands (0 errors, 0 warnings); full-system proof deferred to the merge gate / PR CI
Final-tier runs: 1

Summary: current/1 allocated a temp GIT_INDEX_FILE path but only cleaned it up on the success branch; the error branch piped the failure through cleanup_temp_reason/1, whose every clause was a no-op, so a failing `git add -A` or `git write-tree` leaked the temp index file under .git/specled-tmp. Restructured current/1 to allocate the temp path once via an outer with, delegate the fallible git steps to a new private current_with_index/2, and unconditionally File.rm the temp path before returning the result — mirroring the existing pattern in Sync.write_tree/2 — then dropped the dead cleanup_temp_reason/1. Added a regression test that chmods .git/objects read-only after seeding the index from HEAD so `git add -A` fails mid-flight with the index already on disk, confirmed it fails against the prior implementation (leaked index-* file) and passes against the fix (temp dir empty).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (lib/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash.ex, test/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (sync.ex/entry.ex untouched; git.ex untouched)
- Spec file .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md: not touched (allowed "stubs only, if needed" — the covering stub tagged_tests/tree_hash_mirrors_add_all was already execute:true, so no change needed)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (tagged_tests covering specled.evidence_store.tree_hash_mirrors_add_all — the only execute:true stub the diff bears on), passed: 1
- Requirements coverage:
  - F5 error-path temp-index cleanup: covered by test/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash_test.exs:50. Concrete failure mode caught: a residual `index-*` temp index left in .git/specled-tmp after `git add -A` errors. LOAD-BEARING PROVEN EMPIRICALLY — restored pre-fix lib (git checkout 6836964), ran :50 -> FAILED at `assert File.ls!(tmp_dir) == []` (left: ["index-2-..."], right: []); restored fix -> PASSES. Also reproduced the raw leak via mix run (old code: TMP CONTENTS {:ok, ["index-1-..."]} on the add -A error). Seams: chmod 0o500 on .git/objects + subdirs to force `git add -A` to fail with "insufficient permission for adding an object" AFTER seed_index has already written the temp index to disk. This is filesystem-level fault injection, NOT a mock/stub seam.
  - F5 success-path cleanup (no regression): covered structurally — current/1 now does an unconditional File.rm(index_path) on the shared return path of the outer `with` (bind `result <- current_with_index/2`, always matches), so both the {:ok,_} and {:error,_} branches remove the temp file. Exercised by tree_hash_test.exs:8 (two successful current/1 calls, both green). Note: no explicit empty-tmp assertion on the success path, but File.rm sits on the common return path, so the error-path test transitively proves the success path also removes.
  - Dead function dropped: cleanup_temp_reason/1 removed (grep count 0 in fixed lib; was 3 in pre-fix). No lingering no-op tap.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Diff is limited to the current/1 restructure (outer with allocates temp path, new private current_with_index/2 holds the fallible git steps) + dead-function removal + the regression test/helpers. No F2/F6 behavior change; base/1 and seed_index/2 unchanged.
- Targeted verification: PASS.
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
  - mix format --check-formatted (touched files): clean
  - mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/tree_hash_test.exs: 3 tests, 0 failures
  - mix test --stale: 143 tests, 0 failures
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only): During `mix test --stale` one stray stderr line appeared — `/bin/sh: /var/.../specled_cmd_295.sh: No such file or directory` — from an unrelated command/tagged-verification temp-script race elsewhere in the suite; the run still reported 0 failures and it is not produced by this fix. Not blocking.

title: review-F5: dead cleanup_temp_reason no-op masks temp-index leak on error paths
